chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-701 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case For hearing of main case 16-07-2025 Mr. Abdul Naeem Pirzada, Advcoate along with petitioner. Mr. Akhtiar Hussain, Advocate for Town Committee, Sobhodero. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant A.G. ****** Pursuant to notice, learned counsel for respondent No.5 is present and submits three post-dated cheques bearing No.53105196 dated 16.07.2025, No.53105197 dated 10.08.2025 and No.53105198, dated 10.09.2025 for totaling amounting to Rs.2,85,000/- issued in favour of petitioner; original cheques are handed over to the petitioner against acknowledgment, while its Photostat copy retained on record. Learned AAG and counsel for respondent No.5 submit that payments in terms of averments made in paras No.3&5 have been completed/paid; therefore purpose of petition has been served and therefore seek disposal of the petition. Learned counsel for petitioner as well as petitioner when confronted after receiving cheques submit that they have already made prayer regarding increase of salary as well as pension amount; therefore, per their calculation remaining amount was Rs. 5,52,711/-; however, respondents have brought cheques amounting to Rs.2,85,000/. He, therefore, submits that remaining amount may be directed to be paid to the petitioner. Since petition itself does not show claimed amount; however, per claim of the petitioner in terms of paras No.3&5, Rs. 2,85,499/- has been paid to the petitioner through post-dated cheques today. As far as remaining claim of petitioner is concerned, petitioner is at liberty to approach the respondents for re-calculation and in case of failure, he may exhaust proper remedy, if so advised.

Since petition itself does not show claimed amount; however, per claim of the petitioner in terms of paras No.3&5, Rs. 2,85,499/- has been paid to the petitioner through post-dated cheques today. As far as remaining claim of petitioner is concerned, petitioner is at liberty to approach the respondents for re-calculation and in case of failure, he may exhaust proper remedy, if so advised. Under the circumstances, instant petition has served its purpose; therefore, is hereby disposed of along with pending applications. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
